title: Neoantigen-targeted dendritic cell vaccination in lung cancer patients induces long-lived T cells exhibiting the full differentiation spectrum
population: Human (10 enrolled; vaccine manufactured for 7; 6 vaccinated)
cancer_subtype: NSCLC without EGFR, ALK, ROS1 or RET driver alterations
sample_source: Resected tumour tissue, matched peripheral blood, PBMCs, leukapheresis-derived monocytes, TILs
sequecing_type: WES + bulk RNA-seq + scRNA-seq + single-cell TCR-seq
library_prep: 25-mer tandem minigene (TMG) mRNA-loaded autologous monocyte-derived dendritic cells (Neo-mDCs)
rna_alignment_transcript_tool: kallisto v0.44.0 (transcript quantification); ASEReadCounter (GATK) (variant expression); IGV (manual indel review)
variant_filtering_vaf_threshold: Standard MuTect2 PASS filters; exonic variants retained; final neoantigen selection: clonal variants, binding affinity rank ≤0.5, transcript expression ≥1 TPM, variant expression >0
consequence_focus: Non-synonymous clonal expressed mutations generating neoepitopes
expression_validation: RNA-seq expression quantified by kallisto; variant expression by ASEReadCounter; indels manually reviewed in IGV
neoantigen_prediction_tool: Tumour-normal WES → MuTect2 somatic variant calling → HLA-HD/seq2HLA HLA typing → transcript quantification (kallisto) → variant expression (ASEReadCounter) → MuPeXI prioritization → manual filtering using clonality, expression, LOHHLA, immunopeptidomics and wild-type comparison
binding_affinity_threshold: Binding affinity rank ≤0.5; transcript expression ≥1 TPM; variant expression >0; overlap between NetMHCpan and HLAthena; mutant binding stronger than wild type; mutation preferably central; absent from healthy proteome
neonatigen_identified: Yes (4–6 neoantigens selected per patient; 33 total vaccine neoantigens)
experimental_validation_of_immunogenicit: MS-based immunopeptidomics, engineered B-LCL immunopeptidomics, TIL reactivity assays, intracellular IFN-γ staining, peptide-HLA tetramer staining, flow cytometry, single-cell RNA/TCR sequencing, mutant versus wild-type peptide assays
clonality_estimation: Yes (clonal variant selection; single-cell TCR clonotype analysis showing polyclonal responses)
other_immune_analyses_and_sub_analysis: PureCN (tumour purity); LOHHLA (HLA LOH); HLA LOH assessment; longitudinal PBMC monitoring; CD4/CD8 IFN-γ assays; pHLA tetramer analysis; TSCM analysis; single-cell transcriptomics; pseudotime trajectory analysis; TCR repertoire analysis; exhaustion markers (PDCD1, TOX, HAVCR2); immunopeptidomics; TIL functional assays; long-term immune persistence

title: A Phase Ib Trial of Personalized Neoantigen Therapy Plus Anti-PD-1 in Patients with Advanced Melanoma, Non-small Cell Lung Cancer, or Bladder Cancer
study_type_7: Phase Ib multicenter open-label clinical trial
sample_source: FFPE tumor biopsies, matched normal blood, PBMCs, plasma, serum
library_prep: ACE™ Cancer Research Exome; ACE™ Cancer Research Transcriptome (Personalis)
reference_genome: Gencode v19 (hg19 used for APOE analysis)
rna_alignment_transcript_tool: STAR v2.5.1.b; STAR-Fusion v2.5.1.b; RSEM v1.2.31

variant_filtering_vaf_threshold: Tumor cellularity ≥30%; ensemble filtering using tumor read support, normal reads, orientation bias, coverage, neighbouring variants and read-quality bias; vaccine candidates required ≥50 nonsynonymous mutations/fusions; NetMHCpan rank <2%; expression >10 TPM (expressed mutations) or >5 TPM (frameshifts)
